channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: New Orleans attack came as officials warned of an escalating threat of international terrorism
item.description: The truck attack in New Orleans by an Army veteran inspired by the Islamic State group unfolded amid months of warnings by officials about a resurgent terrorism threat.
item.dc:creator: Eric Tucker, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump's sentencing is set for Jan. 10. Here's what could happen next
item.description: The judge in President-elect Donald Trump’s hush money case has made a dramatic decision that could nevertheless bring the case to a muted end.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Peltz And Michael R. Sisak, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Former FBI agent: Transparency could stop conspiracy theorists
item.description: Gallagher said that while misinformation is "always a danger" in national events like these, "the FBI deals in evidence. Conspiracy theorists don't.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: President Biden awards Medal of Honor to seven Army veterans
item.description: President Biden has awarded the highest military decoration in the nation to seven U.S. Army veterans for their heroism in the Vietnam and Korean Wars.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump reclaims influence of GOP as Republicans fall in line behind Johnson
item.description: The House speaker vote was a litmus test of President-elect Trump's ability to unify a fractious Republican Party.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jeffries claims 'no election deniers' among Dems despite 2016 'illegitimate' remarks when Trump won
item.description: House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries, D-N.Y., who previously claimed that the 2016 presidential election was "illegitimate," told lawmakers that there are "no election deniers" in the Democratic Party.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 23-year-old hopeful files to run for Tampa mayor
item.description: The next mayoral race is not until 2027. For Alan Henderson, he hopes that’s enough time for people to know his name and what he hopes to accomplish, if elected.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Ohio governor vetoes medical free speech clause he says would gut state's regulatory power
item.description: Ohio Gov. Mike DeWine has vetoed part of a bill cast by state lawmakers as protecting the medical free speech of doctors and other health professionals.
item.dc:creator: Julie Carr Smyth, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump slams Merchan, Democrats, who 'just want to see if they can get a pound of flesh' amid failed cases
item.description: EXCLUSIVE: President-elect Donald Trump slammed Judge Juan Merchan for denying his request to dismiss the charges against him in New York v. Trump, telling Fox News Digital Democrats “just want to see if they can get a pound of flesh because every case has failed.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: GOP lawmaker, 81, receives medical attention after fall at US Capitol
item.description: Rep. Virginia Foxx of North Carolina, the Republican House Education Committee chair, fell on on a staircase at the U.S. Capitol Friday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: GOP rebels switch vote to Johnson after Trump’s 11th hour calls, pushing him over the finish line
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson was re-elected to lead the House of Representatives after a dramatic floor vote Friday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Johnson battles for the Speakership: 5 things to watch
item.description: Friday’s Speaker vote is shaping up to be a nail-biter as Rep. Mike Johnson (R-La.), the current House leader, is scrambling to keep the gavel for the next two years in the face of conservative critics furious with his leadership style.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Biden awards Medal of Honor to seven US Army soldiers
item.description: President Biden on Friday awarded the Medal of Honor to seven U.S. Army soldiers, most of whom received it posthumously for service during the Korea War, in his last ceremony as commander-in-chief awarding the country’s highest military honor.
item.dc:creator: The Hill, NewsNation

item.title: AP Decision Notes: What to expect in Virginia's state legislative special elections
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Voters in Virginia will begin the new year filling state Senate and House vacancies on Tuesday in a trio of special elections that could determine control of the narrowly divided chambers. In the Washington suburbs of Loudoun County, Democratic state Sen.
item.dc:creator: ROBERT YOON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Here’s what Congress’s first day will look like
item.description: The 119th Congress kicks off Friday as lawmakers return to Washington with a long January to-do list and a packed first day. Confirmation hearings for President-elect Trump’s Cabinet nominees are set to get rolling in the coming weeks, and Trump himself will be inaugurated in less than three weeks.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden awards the Medal of Honor and Medal of Valor to military heroes and first responders
item.description: President Joe Biden has awarded the Medal of Honor to military recipients who displayed heroism and self-sacrifice in combat and the Medal of Valor to first responders who put their own lives at risk to save others.
item.dc:creator: Josh Boak, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump to rally in DC ahead of inauguration
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — Donald Trump is expected to hold a big Washington rally the day before he's sworn in as the nation's 47th president. The victory rally, as it's being billed, will be held at the Capitol One Arena in the District of Columbia on Jan. 19.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bill would exempt Tennessee SROs from immigration status reporting requirement
item.description: A new bill sponsored by a Republican senator would shield school resource officers from having to report a student's immigration status to the federal government in hopes of protecting children in schools.
item.dc:creator: Tori Gessner, NewsNation

item.title: Chief Justice Roberts condemns elected officials for intimidating judges
item.description: Chief Justice John Roberts condemned elected officials who have intimidated judges and defied court rulings, highlighting them in his year-end report Tuesday as part of what Roberts called “illegitimate activity” that threatens the rule of law.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Trump calls it the 'center of the universe.' Mar-a-Lago is a magnet for those seeking influence
item.description: PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — The cars begin lining up early in the morning to be screened by Secret Service agents under white tents near the fence that surrounds President-elect Donald Trump’s vast south Florida estate.
item.dc:creator: ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON and MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Kevin Cramer’s son sentenced to 28 years over chase, fatal car crash
item.description: The son of North Dakota Sen. Kevin Cramer (R) was sentenced Monday to 28 years in prison over a police chase and death of a local sheriff’s deputy. Ian Cramer, 43, was charged with manslaughter last December after he crashed his family’s SUV into a police cruiser, concluding an hourlong chase.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Imperial Beach mayor vying for San Diego County Supervisor
item.description: After San Diego County Board of Supervisors Chair Nora Vargas announced her decision to vacate her position at the end of her current term, three people have announced their desire to sit in her place.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, Fox 5

item.title: Trump gives complete and total endorsement of Johnson as Speaker
item.description: President-elect Trump offered his complete and total endorsement of Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) on Monday, days before a House vote on Friday to elect a new Speaker. "Speaker Mike Johnson is a good, hard working, religious man. He will do the right thing, and we will continue to WIN.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, WFLA, NBC
